name: Stregheria
description: Stregheria, meaning 'witchcraft' in Italian, is a modern Pagan religion and tradition inspired by Charles Godfrey Leland's book 'Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches'. While claiming to revive an ancient Etruscan-Roman religion of witchcraft, its historical accuracy is debated, with scholars arguing it's a synthesis of folklore, historical accounts of witchcraft trials, and Leland's own interpretations. Stregheria emphasizes a balance between the God and Goddess, often referred to as Diana and Lucifer (in their pre-Christian light-bearing forms), representing the Queen of Heaven and the Horned God. Practices include spellcraft, herbalism, divination (tarot, scrying), and reverence for ancestors and household spirits. Celebrations often center around the Wheel of the Year, adapted from Wiccan and other Pagan traditions. Stregheria stresses personal responsibility, ethical practice, and a connection to the land and its spirits. Covens are common but solitary practice is also accepted. The tradition has evolved significantly since Leland's initial work, with diverse interpretations and practices among contemporary Strega practitioners. It emphasizes personal gnosis and adaptation within the framework of its core principles, making it a dynamic and evolving spiritual path. The tradition often incorporates elements of Italian folk magic and healing practices, creating a unique blend of ancient and modern influences.
subtitle: Italian Witchcraft: Ancient Roots, Modern Practice

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Diana
keyMyths.description: Diana, the Queen of the Witches, is said to have been born before time, a powerful and independent goddess. She represents the wildness and freedom of nature.
keyMyths.source: Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches

keyMyths.title: Lucifer as Light-Bearer
keyMyths.description: In Stregheria, Lucifer is not equated with the Christian Devil but is seen as a god of light, knowledge, and rebellion against oppression. He is often depicted as a bringer of wisdom.

sources.author: Charles Godfrey Leland
sources.work: Aradia, or the Gospel of the Witches
sources.description: A foundational text claiming to document an ancient Italian witchcraft tradition.
sources.text: A foundational text claiming to document an ancient Italian witchcraft tradition.

sources.author: Raven Grimassi
sources.description: A modern interpretation and reconstruction of Stregheria.
